subject: Mathematics
title: Sherlock Holmes and the Logic of Detection
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Sherlock Holmes and the Logic of Detection.

hook.text: 'When you eliminate the impossible, whatever remains must be the truth.' Holmes said it. Is he right?

buildup.text: Holmes claims to use deduction. But most of his reasoning is actually abduction—guessing the best explanation.

discovery.text: True deduction would require knowing all the rules in advance. Detectives rarely have that luxury.

twist.text: Eliminating the impossible assumes you've listed every possibility—an assumption that often fails in reality.

climax.text: Real forensic science combines deduction, induction, and abduction—not just one method.

punchline.text: Even the world's greatest detective misnamed his own method.

quiz.question: What type of reasoning does Sherlock Holmes actually use most?
